text:
Feelings × Hopelessness × The Spider's Downfall (想い×断念×引き裂かれたクモ, Omoi × Dannen × Hikisakareta Kumo) is the 70 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 8th episode of the Yorknew City OVA series. It was released on DVD on April 17 th , 2002.

Overview

Since Chrollo's Nen is constricted, Hisoka lets go of his dream of fighting Chrollo for the time being. Pakunoda transfers her memories to six Troupe members and, as a result, dies for not obeying the chain's rules.

Synopsis

On the airship, Kurapika finally answers his cell phone and is greeted by Hisoka. Kurapika, as well as the others present to make the exchange for the hostages, is surprised and angered by Hisoka's sudden appearance as he comes into view on the tarmac below. Over the phone, Hisoka threatens to hurt Gon and Killua if Kurapika doesn't let him fight Chrollo. However, he is only bluffing about hurting the kids. 

Hisoka joins Pakunoda, Gon, and Killua on their airship, which follows alongside Kurapika's to their destination. When both airships eventually land, everyone gets out, and Kurapika calls Killua on his cell phone, instructing him to put his phone up to his heart. Killua does as told, and Melody listens to his heartbeat over the phone to ensure nothing bad happened with them and Hisoka on the way over. When she gives Kurapika the all-clear, Kurapika announces that the hostage exchange will be made, and Chrollo, Gon, and Killua begin walking.

Once Gon and Killua have gotten over halfway towards Kurapika's group, Kurapika begins to run towards them, and Leorio and Melody worry that he may do something rash and rush to stop him. However, when Kurapika looks back at Leorio and Melody, the scarlet color of his eyes has faded away, and he cheerfully tells them not to worry, much to Leorio and Melody's surprise. Kurapika runs to Gon and Killua with Leorio and Melody in tow, and the group is happily reunited. On the other side, Chrollo walks by Pakunoda towards their airship without saying a word while Pakunoda watches the joyful reunion happening on Kurapika's side.

Once Kurapika's airship has left and only the small group of Spiders remain, Hisoka exclaims how long he has waited to challenge Chrollo. He then whips off his shirt and reveals to Chrollo that the Spider tattoo on his back had been a fake all along. However, Chrollo smiles and informs Hisoka that he can no longer fight. Initially, Hisoka thinks that Chrollo is joking or scheming something. Chrollo clarifies that he can no longer use Nen because of Kurapika, but Hisoka still thinks that Chrollo is joking until he tries to attack Chrollo, and Chrollo does nothing to stop him. Hisoka gets on the airship with Pakunoda, depressed that his longtime dream has come to an unsatisfying end. Their airship departs after Chrollo instructs them to return to base and leave him behind. 

On the other airship, Gon is excited that the head of the Spiders has been essentially incapacitated, as he believes this will bring about the end of the Spiders and mean that Kurapika will no longer feel the need to go after them. Kurapika, however, is certain that the Spiders will continue without their leader and refuses to stop pursuing them. Melody senses that something is wrong with Kurapika right before Kurapika collapses. 

As Hisoka and Pakunoda part ways, Hisoka informs Pakunoda that he has actually been lying about the prophecy he received and how differently things were supposed to play out. Yet, somehow, their fates had been changed. Pakunoda wanders back through the city alone until she encounters a stray kitten in an alley, which she quickly befriends. While reminiscing on her times with Chrollo when they were younger, the kitten leads Pakunoda to a park where many stray cats have gathered in a group together. Pakunoda fondly says goodbye to the kitten, happy to leave them with their companions before returning to the Spiders' hideout to meet with hers.

When she meets back up with the other Spiders, Phinks immediately interrogates her about what happened to Chrollo, and the group worries about why Pakunoda is holding a pistol in her hand. She promises to tell them everything with the use of her Memory Bomb, but is only able to use six bullets for the six original members of the Troupe. While Phinks worries that Pakunoda is under the control of Kurapika, Nobunaga insists that they trust her, and Pakunoda fires the six bullets. The six members of the Phantom Troupe obtain her memories. Still, due to the conditions of Kurapika's Nen chain under which Pakunoda agreed never to use her Nen again, Pakunoda is immediately killed.
